title: Habits over one-time things — Yahir's 19-year-old self-audit
html:
Habits over one-time things — Yahir's 19-year-old self-audit

Source: 2022-06-04 Apple Note. A short habits self-audit at 8:52am — Yahir at 19/20, end of OSU spring term, listing his bad and good habits.

TL;DR

  • Thesis: "One time things don't really matter, what matters in the end is the habits that one has/constructs." Compounding-over-burst frame, written with no qualification.
  • Bad habits named: lie about himself; bite nails; "rely on others for action" — and he tags two specific examples: "Gym Owen" (dependent on Owen for going to the gym) and "playing stuff with Yermy" (dependent on his brother Yermy for activity initiation).
  • Maybe-bad habit (with hesitation): "feel like I need to always improve" — flagged with a question mark. Yahir is already aware that his constant-improvement instinct may itself be a pathology. Predates by years his later fear of not doing frame.
  • Good habits: "Can say no more than most"; "When I'm determined it's pretty hard to stop me"; "Keep my family in mind (drive)"; "Aware that our time is limited here at OSU and in life, for the most part…?.?.?.?".
  • The ellipsis tail on the family-and-mortality good-habit shows uncertainty — not full conviction. Yahir is performing the awareness, not yet living it. The repeated question marks are the tell.

Pattern observed

This is the earliest crisp self-audit in the corpus — the same genre as his later thoughts and things docs but stripped to its bones. The "rely on others for action" callout naming Owen specifically is interesting — it predates Owen's later role as the longest-running roommate-friend in the Austin arc.

The "feel like I need to always improve" doubt is the first appearance of the fear of not doing worry — at 19, Yahir already suspects his ambition is partly compulsion. He won't articulate this cleanly until 2024-2025, but the seed is here.

Key quotes

  • "One time things don't really matter, what matters in the end is the habits that one has/constructs."
  • "*maybe bad habit (feel like I need to always improve)."
  • "Aware that our time is limited here at OSU and in life, for the most part….?.?.?.?"

related.label: 12/19/2025 — three jobs no failures, Rankett-is-it, make something from nothing
